title: German Beef Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful German-inspired stew made with tender beef, vegetables, and a rich broth.
instructions: Heat the irish butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the beef from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomato to the pot and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the xtra beef broth, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Return the beef to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Add the stew and bay leaves to the pot and cook for an additional 30 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley if desired.
